pet.name: rtyutyr
pet.special_needs: Needs secure supervised enrichment, feeding consistency, hydration monitoring, and close observation of stress, appetite, and handling tolerance.
pet.characteristics: Raccoons in captive/companion settings require secure, enriched environments with climbing structures, foraging opportunities, and puzzle feeders to support natural behaviors. Feeding should follow a consistent schedule with varied whole foods; hydration monitoring is essential as they are prone to dehydration. Handling tolerance varies widely by individual and early socialization; stress indicators include excessive vocalization, aggression, or appetite loss and warrant immediate observation. They are intelligent, dexterous, and escape-prone, so enclosures must be secure and regularly inspected. Environmental enrichment, regular health monitoring, and minimal stressors support behavioral stability. Nocturnal activity patterns should be accommodated.
pet.species_profile_summary: Raccoons typically live 12-16 years in captive settings and require secure, enriched environments with consistent feeding, close behavioral monitoring, and careful handling protocols.

age_context.summary: Raccoons typically live 12-16 years in captive settings and require secure, enriched environments with consistent feeding, close behavioral monitoring, and careful handling protocols.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: A senior pet plan should prioritize consistency over intensity. When time is limited, essential care such as feeding, hydration, medication reminders, and mobility support should be scheduled before optional enrichment.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Daily care plans are stronger when they combine essential care with realistic enrichment and observation. Short play, sniff walks, brushing, or calm interaction can improve quality of life without requiring large blocks of time.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Pets on kidney-support diets often need strict consistency around meals and hydration. Wet-food feeding schedules, water refreshes, and appetite monitoring are common routine tasks that owners can track day by day.

raw_recommendations.name: Morning feeding and hydration check
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer consistent whole-food meal and fresh water; monitor appetite and intake.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Raccoons require consistent feeding schedules and hydration monitoring to prevent dehydration and support stable behavior.

raw_recommendations.name: Evening feeding and hydration check
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer consistent whole-food meal and refresh water; note any changes in appetite or behavior.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Second daily feeding maintains consistency and allows evening hydration monitoring; raccoons are nocturnal so evening feeding aligns with natural activity.

raw_recommendations.name: Behavioral and appetite observation
raw_recommendations.notes: Record stress indicators, vocalization, appetite refusal, aggression, or handling tolerance changes.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Short, specific observation tasks create a record of appetite, energy, and stress for early detection of health or behavioral concerns.

raw_recommendations.name: Enrichment and puzzle feeder activity
raw_recommendations.notes: Offer puzzle feeder, foraging opportunity, or climbing structure to support natural behaviors.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Intelligent, dexterous raccoons need daily enrichment and foraging opportunities to reduce stress and support behavioral stability.

raw_recommendations.name: Enclosure security and escape-risk inspection
raw_recommendations.notes: Check for gaps, loose latches, or damage; raccoons are escape-prone and require secure, regularly inspected enclosures.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Raccoons are intelligent and dexterous; regular enclosure inspection prevents escape and ensures safe, secure environment.

raw_recommendations.name: Water system cleaning and refresh
raw_recommendations.notes: Clean water bowl or system and provide fresh water to prevent dehydration and contamination.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Raccoons are prone to dehydration; regular water system maintenance supports consistent hydration monitoring.

raw_recommendations.name: Stress and handling tolerance assessment
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe response to gentle interaction; note any aggression, excessive vocalization, or avoidance indicating stress.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Handling tolerance varies widely by individual; consistent monitoring supports safe interaction and early stress detection.
